    1. injectFields(T, Map)

Class BeanProvider

java.lang.Object
com.github.hazendaz.beanprovider.BeanProvider
@Vetoed public final class BeanProvider extends Object
Customized BeanProvider which allows to perform field-injection on non-CDI managed classes.

This class contains utility methods to resolve contextual references in situations where no injection is available because the current class is not managed by the CDI Container. This can happen in e.g. a JPA-2.0 EntityListener, a ServletFilter, a Spring managed Bean, etc.

Attention: This method is intended for being used in user code at runtime. If this method gets used during Container boot (in an Extension), non-portable behavior results. The CDI specification only allows injection of the BeanManager during CDI-Container boot time.

  • Method Summary

    Method
    Description
    static <T> T
    injectFields(T instance, Map<String, Class<? extends Annotation>> ignoreMap)
    Allows to perform dependency injection for instances which aren't managed by CDI.

    Methods inherited from class Object

    clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

  • Method Details

    • injectFields

      public static <T> T injectFields(T instance, Map<String, Class<? extends Annotation>> ignoreMap)
      Allows to perform dependency injection for instances which aren't managed by CDI.

      Attention: The resulting instance isn't managed by CDI; only fields annotated with Inject (or marked with PostInject and then synthesized to Inject) get initialized.

      Type Parameters:
      T - current type
      Parameters:
      instance - current instance (required)
      ignoreMap - map of field names to annotation types which should be removed from those fields (required)
      Returns:
      the same instance with injected fields
